affordances.name: Commitment evidence revision check
affordances.mechanism: When a public, costly, or identity-linked commitment is being defended against contrary evidence, require the answer to separate evidence updating from comfort-restoring belief revision and add pre-set exit or review criteria.

use_when: A person or team is defending a prior commitment after contrary signals appear.
The commitment is public, ego-identified, costly, or hard to reverse.
Evidence avoidance, rationalization, or escalation may be preserving psychological consistency.
do_not_use_when: The discomfort is simply a useful sign of unresolved uncertainty and no commitment defense is visible.
The case lacks evidence of prior commitment, identity threat, or contrary signals.
A better fit is purely financial sunk-cost analysis or operational bottleneck diagnosis.
case_evidence_needed: The prior commitment and who is identified with it.
The contrary signal being minimized or avoided.
The current rationalization or escalation behavior.
Any kill criteria, leading indicators, or independent review path.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the discomfort-preserving commitment, the evidence being defended against, and a review mechanism that makes changing course socially and psychologically cheaper.
evidence_required: Predefined criteria for continuing, changing, or stopping.
good_output_shape: Distinguishes honest belief updating from comfort-restoring rationalization.
Asks what evidence would make the prior commitment wrong.
Adds kill criteria or independent review before more commitment.
Protects public mind-changing as good judgment, not humiliation.

diagnostic_questions: What commitment would become uncomfortable if this evidence were taken seriously?
Are we revising beliefs toward accuracy or toward consistency with what we already did?
What would we do if this same project or position were brought fresh today?
misuse_guards: Do not treat all discomfort as cognitive dissonance.
Do not use the label to psychoanalyze motives without commitment and evidence-avoidance signals.
Do not resolve the tension by urging consistency; the source frames the risk as motivated belief revision.

source_evidence.source_quote: people do not change behavior to align with beliefs nearly as often as they change beliefs to align with behavior
a public, ego-identified commitment has been made
Apply explicit kill criteria before committing resources

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports dissonance as post-commitment belief revision requiring exit criteria and independent review.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic discomfort management.
Did not convert cognitive dissonance into motive certainty.
open_questions: Later packet review should test overlap with commitment-bias and sunk-cost records.

absence_records.reason: The source treats dissonance as a drive toward motivated resolution, not proof that the uncomfortable belief or action is wrong.

source_evidence.source_quote: motivated belief revision, not a mechanism for honest belief updating

absence_records.reason: The source supports belief revision through commitments and behavior; it does not support claiming a private attitude changed without evidence of the commitment context.

source_evidence.source_quote: The public advocacy creates a cognition

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 14 source reading found strong reviewed depth for cognitive dissonance as a commitment-review and evidence-avoidance guard.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should decide when this card or commitment-bias should carry the sharper treatment.
